rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On First Amended Petition For Approval Of Transfer Of Structured Settlement Payment Rights
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Friday, May 09, 2025, Line 9. PETITIONER TBT SERSVICES, LLC Notice Of Hearing On First Amended Petition For Approval Of Transfer Of Structured Settlement Payment Rights. Petitioner TBT Services, LLC's First Amended Petition For Approval Of Transfer Of Structured Settlement Payment Rights is DENIED. (The Court's complete tentative ruling has been emailed to the parties). For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: REGINA CORBIN VS. DOORDASH, INC., A DELAWARE COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Dismissal Of Lawsuit Pursuant To Cal. Code Civ. Procedure 581
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Friday, May 09, 2025, Line 3. PLAINTIFF REGINA CORBIN's Motion For Dismissal Of Lawsuit Pursuant To Cal. Code Civ. Procedure 581. Plaintiff Regina Corbin's unopposed Motion For Dismissal Of Lawsuit Pursuant To Cal. Code Civ. Procedure 581 is DENIED. The court will not authorize dismissal under Code of Civil Procedure section 581(b)(1) absent more information, in light of the fact that dismissal under this statute generally does not require a court order. The motion makes no showing why a court order is necessary or appropriate. Further, this is a class action and it appears the request for dismissal is based on a settlement. A settlement of a class action or class claims requires court approval. (See California Rules of Court, rule 3.769.) Dismissal of a class action requires court approval. (See California Rules of Court, rule 3.770.) For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: CAILIN HARDELL VS. BLUMBERG CAPITAL ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Discovery Of Documents And Information From Defendant Blumberg Capital L.L.C.
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Friday, May 09, 2025, Line 4. 3 - PLAINTIFF CAILIN HARDELL's Motion To Compel Further Discovery Of Documents And Information From Defendant Blumberg Capital L.L.C. Plaintiff Cailin Hardell's Motion to Compel is DENIED. Plaintiff's RFP no. 34 as narrowed is reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence. (See CCP Section 2017.010.) Defendant provided a response. The record does not demonstrate the response was evasive or incomplete. If Plaintiff had not agreed to narrow the request, the court would have sustained the overbreadth objection. If the court cannot rely on the representations of members of this Bar (particularly in light of no evidence that such reliance would be misplaced), then heaven help us. The motion fails. Sanctions denied. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: CAILIN HARDELL VS. BLUMBERG CAPITAL ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion & Motion To Compel Discovery Responses From Plaintiff
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Friday, May 09, 2025, Line 4. 1 - DEFENDANTs BLUMBERG CAPITAL, L.L.C., BLUMBERG CAPITAL V., L.P.,and BLUMBERG CAPITAL MANAGEMENT V, L.L.C.'s Motion To Compel Discovery Responses From Plaintiff. Defendant Blumberg Capital LLC's Motion to Compel Further Discovery is DENIED. Meet and confer was adequate. The discovery requests contained in RFP nos 59, 60, 61 & 62 and SROGS 7 & 12 may be reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence extending this concept to its outer limit. (See CCP Section 2017.010.) For example, some of responsive information may be admissible as impeachment should Mohsen or Vanzyl testify. The court, however, need not stretch this analysis further. Plaintiff Cailin Hardell has demonstrated the merit of her privilege and privacy objections. Settlement discussions are private and/or privileged; they are important to the ends of settlement; and courts have a strong interest in encouraging negotiated resolutions of cases. Defendant did not establish interests in disclosure that overcome Plaintiff's interest in non-disclosure, even in light of the Civil Discovery Act's broad rules favoring disclosure. The same analysis applies to the settlement agreements themselves. This ruling is without prejudice to amotion directed at the trial judge should a specific need for the information arise at trial. Defendant Urban Outfitters, Inc. has not established that it has provided any responses to the form interrogatories. DEFENDANT IS ADVISED stacked discovery motions are strongly disfavored. Going forward, stacked motions will be ordered off calendar in favor of individual motions focused each on a single discovery vehicle (e.g., Frogs, SRogs, RFPs, RFAs, deposition notice) addressed to a single, specific party. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: GREGORY WRIGHT VS. NASRI GEORGE SAMARA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: APPLICATION AND HEARING FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Friday, May 09, 2025, Line 8. PLAINTIFF GREGORY WRIGHT , AN INDIVIDUAL APPLICATION AND HEARING FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER. Plaintiff Gregory Wright's Motion for Writ of Attachment and Application For Right To Attach Order is GRANTED. The court finds Plaintiff has made the showing required by Code of Civil Procedure section 484.090(a). The court finds a writ of attachment should issue under Code of Civil Procedure section 483.010. Defendant Nasri George Samara failed to overcome Wright's showing on the merits of the claim on which he has already secured summary adjudication. Samara has failed to demonstrate the amount sought to be secured should be reduced by the amount of any indebtedness of Wright to Samra that Samara has claimed by way of the cross-complaint. Nor has Samara demonstrated a right of exemption as to any asset subject to the attachment writ/order. Plaintiff has a right to attach property of Defendant Samara in the amount of $716,136. A writ of attachment shall issue, accordingly. An undertaking of $20,000 is required before a writ shall issue. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing.
